human_verification.notes: Reviewed the ambulatory-and-physician-services package for ambulatory workflow specificity, CMS and AHRQ source quality, physician-practice and ASC realism, measure and survey boundary discipline, adjacent-lane routing, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to ambulatory quality evidence review, outpatient quality-measure workpaper handling, ASC survey and infection-control evidence, patient-experience review, hold or reopening boundary discipline, and corrective-action packaging.

Authority sources cover current CMS ambulatory surgery, ASCQR, QPP, and eCQM references plus AHRQ ambulatory safety-culture, safety toolkit, and CAHPS materials that materially change terminology, artifacts, systems, and refusal posture for ambulatory-quality work.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete evidence, repeat defects, control-bypass requests, ambulatory-specific prerequisite gaps, unresolved holds, incident escalation, adjacent peer-review and compliance routing, policy conflict, tenant adaptation, and unsupported closure attempts.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/ambulatory-and-physician-services/ambulatory-quality-specialist/ to preserve the canonical NAICS-derived overlay path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around clinical judgment, peer review, final reporting attestation, legal interpretation, privacy investigation, and reopening authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated ambulatory quality overlay where final clinical, peer-review, compliance, accreditation, and reporting authority remain external.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
